title: How to get Projects and Dependencies to skip over weekends
excerpt: I have my gantt chart set to not show weekends not sure how to turn weekends off on calendar if possible? I have projects linked with set amounts of work days on each. How do I get them to skip over weekends when I have …

posts.author: jmay (JOSHUA MAY)
posts.cookedHtml: I have my gantt chart set to not show weekends not sure how to turn weekends off on calendar if possible? I have projects linked with set amounts of work days on each. How do I get them to skip over weekends when I have them linked as dependencies and adjust start end date?

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jmay

To have dependencies skip weekends entirely, you will need to have an Admin go and change the timeline setting for the entire account.

In the Admin Settings, head to the “Account” tab in the “General” section. From here, you can choose whether you want to hide or display weekends in your timeline! If you make any changes, be sure to hit “Save changes” before you navigate away from the page.

image1521×942 144 KB

This will affect all Timeline Column features, but it will NOT hide weekends for The Date Column.

I hope this helps! Remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Cheers,
Jenna

posts.author: cmg35 (Christine Gotts)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Jenna,

This week the Timeline Weekends selection has also been applied to the Date Column on our boards. Toggling the Timeline Weekends on and off also toggles weekends available to select in the Date Column. Is this a bug or a deliberate change? We need to be able to select weekends in the Date Column and exclude them in the Timeline Column.

Thanks
Christine

posts.author: marcy (Marcy M.)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Christine - This also happened on our account. Have you heard anything from monday.com? We reached out to support with no real answers other than to change the setting in Admin. I did so, reluctantly and am regretting it ever since.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @jmay , @cmg35 , @marcy

If you are looking for a planning solution (with dependencies) skipping over weekend AND defined holiday I suggest to have a look at the SchedulerPro app (see https://monday.com/marketplace/10000139). The app provides a view where you can define the working days and the non-working dates. Like this:

image1122×1060 62.2 KB

The scheduling part of the app supports all dependencies types (forward and backward planning), lead and lag times and more.

posts.author: cmg35 (Christine Gotts)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bas,

Thanks for the suggestion but I’m not looking for an app. I’m looking for the core functionality of the board and in particular the date column settings to be restored to their previous working set-up.

The Timeline Weekends setting should not affect the Date column as the functions are completely separate. It worked beautifully before - simple but effective - the change has been a major headache.

Thanks
Christine

Thanks
Christine

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml: This is really insane that this feature is not a core feature. Monday needs to step it up ASAP. Super low hanging fruit, easy to do. I am very frustrated by this, the visual real estate it takes up is alot.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone

We are currently releasing an app which enables you to set your projects to skip over weekends and other defined dates. Also, here are some of the features of the app:

Built-in holiday checker for most countries
Ability to define holidays for users in your account
Set non-working days
Support for native monday automations
Date and timeline support
Support for teams holidays
And so much more…

To get early access to the app, please fill this form - Date Check - Early Access

Here is a demo of the app in action.

If you have any questions, you can always reach out at support@kolaai.com

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally cool that we have multiple apps available to fix a glaring issue created by Monday, however Monday needs to fix this issue. Not hope for a third party developer to band aid over the issue. Be aware, when apps are designed to fix issues created by Monday, Monday loses the impetus to actually fix their issues.

posts.author: FrancisElliott (Francis Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would agree that this should be at least one of the following:

  • Account setting separated for Timeline/Date columns
  • Board setting
  • Column setting for all Timeline/Date columns

Many organizations may have one department that works on a 7-day week, while another works on a 5-day week.

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml:
I totally agree. This is such a easy fix, and yet it has been on Monday’s radar since 2020. This is not going to get fixed by Monday. I cant imagine how small their development team is that cant make this happen. They already have the code for it, you can make weekends go away on the Gantt charts. Multiple clients, when I have sent the project calendar to, have asked why we work on weekends. I have had to explain that the management software cannot handle this. It is embarrassing for myself and for Monday.

Please fix this ASAP!

posts.author: achakraborty (Agniva)
posts.cookedHtml: I am facing the same issue. Does anyone know if this issue has been fixed yet? Any suggestion will be very helpful.

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml: Currently the “fix” is to spend between 100-300$ a month on a third party extension. What a massive failure. Maybe a design feature…

Gbolyard:

monthly cost of app1039×603 33.4 KB

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Posting to bring this back up to front page. This needs to be addressed by Monday, not sorta solved by a third party app I have to pay money for.

Fix this PLEASE?!

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Joseph, you have a great idea, however here is a fundamental issue.

When a dependency changes on the timeline, it potentially shifts everything else. That means, things that were not in the weekend, are now in the weekend. So now I am manually changing things to move to the following Monday. This completely defeats the purpose of automation.

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bumping this thread again to keep Monday on task. This is a easy issue to deal with and obviously people want this dealt with.
Monday continues to ignore the most basic fixes that would bring tremendous quality of life to their software.

posts.author: WilliamdR (Willliam del Rivero)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just adding to this thread for Monday.com to perform this increadibly easy upgrade. It doesn’t make any sense that you can’t choose to hide/display weekends at a board or column level.
Everytime I raise an issue in this forum the solution is either purchase an additional app or change the whole work.

posts.author: gillian_nation (Gillian Nation)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding to the thread that this functionality is absolutely required for baseline project management software. We were promised this feature during sales pitches and what they have released has zero utility. We even tested above 3rd party app which is not useful (it shortens task duration instead of skipping the blocked out time) and only worked even in that capacity 25% of the time.

posts.author: mmindling (Madeleine Mindling)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding to this thread as well. This would be such a useful functionality, especially since our content often goes live on the weekends, so on our Post Date column we want to include weekends, but we only work on these projects and estimate the timelines based on work weeks, so want to exclude weekends from our project timelines when estimating for clients.

posts.author: PM_Karen (Karen H)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding to this topic as well. This is an essential feature that should be part of the native platform and not requiring a 3rd party plug in.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is exactly the solution I am looking for!
Have a timeline column without weekends but schedule release dates on weekends.

posts.author: JB007 (JulesWarner)

Has Monday.com put this basic functionality in to the core product yet or is this still creating a market for 3rd party software companies to put in something you’d reasonably expect anyway?

It’s like buying a car then having to pay extra for one with an engine!

posts.author: Roger-T (Roger Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: We are also having this issue. Bumping the thread again to hopefully get some attention. Agree totally with @FrancisElliott it should be available at all levels. Even if was a Workspace setting this would help enormously.

posts.author: rtwise (Ryan Wise)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve started our business on trial with Monday & just ran into this as an issue. We run a 4 day working week and the inability for us to customise our calendar around this is annoying enough that I would consider cancelling our account and running different software. Otherwise the software has been good so far, has anyone had any contact about a potential fix from Monday.com? We are also not interested in a 3rd party add on.

posts.author: gillian_nation (Gillian Nation)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve posted above but this issue needs to be fixed. I’ve tested the third party apps - none of which work. This is costing our company money at this point

posts.author: jwebster (Jane W)
posts.cookedHtml: We are also looking for a solution to this problem without resorting to third-party apps. We have several boards with numerous items with finish-to-start dependent timelines. Manually adjusting all of those to account for weekends makes the dependencies far less useful.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
I constantly read that people (just like me) do not want to pay for third party solutions. Has monday ever considered to make them free for subscribers and pay the developers of third party apps for their solutions that monday itself did not have time to develop themselves?
This would definitely make them more attractive and take pressure from monday.

posts.author: BigWaves (Joanne)
posts.cookedHtml: Nearly November, no update since promising one in April. I’m new to Monday and relieved I only purchased month-to-month, as this is such an obvious showstopper and appears to not be seen as such by Monday? What do people use this for, nicely formatted notes? It’s marketed as a project management tool (among other things), and bedrock of that is setting what your team’s workdays are and are not so that dependencies know which days are available for the durations set. This is not something for an app, this is core. We certainly won’t be porting any more projects into this new tool until we learn where this functionality is on the roadmap.

posts.author: csorum (csorum@habitatmetrodenver.org)
posts.cookedHtml: Following this as we need a fix as well. We have some staff that work Tuesday - Saturday, some that work Monday - Friday, and some that work Tuesday - Sunday. We need to be able to choose what working days are for the timelines and dependencies to be useful.

posts.author: jram (Jacob)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow! I am so glad that I found this thread before switching my entire team and project over to Monday. It’s so disappointing to see that not only is a key feature like this ignored after being requested for YEARS, but that the solution from Monday is to let third parties rack up additional costs for their users. Feel like we dodged a bullet here!

posts.author: FrancisElliott (Francis Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: Actually this feature has been implemented now I believe. I’m seeing it across the accounts I manage.

posts.author: csorum (csorum@habitatmetrodenver.org)
posts.cookedHtml: It hasn’t been resolved at a board level, only at an account level. And on the account level it only lets you choose Monday - Sunday or Monday - Friday schedules, it doesn’t let you customize days. Unless you have seen otherwise?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: The app SchedulerPro support configurable workdays that can be used per board. There can be multiple workday and holiday schedules and at the board level you can pick which schedule to use.
